entry.title: ABAP RAP - Action is not Updating the UI (unmanaged / managed)
entry.content:
Hello Community

I am currently trying to fill a field with a different value using an action. To do this, I create a READ entity to determine the selected rows, then I change the field and return it using the Result statement.

Unfortunately, the UI is not updated so that the new value appears in the column.

I have stored my action as follows:
action load result [1] $self;

My action method looks like this:

READ ENTITIES OF XXXX IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY YYYYY
FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
RESULT DATA(lt_rows)
FAILED failed.

LOOP AT lt_rows ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<row>).
<row>-FIELD1 = ‘NEW_TEXT’.
<row>-FIELD2 = ‘NEW TEXT 2’.
INSERT VALUE #( %tky = <row>-%tky %param = <row> ) INTO TABLE result.
ENDLOOP.

Regardless of whether I try it in a managed or unmanaged environment, it does not work. Could someone assist me in identifying what the problem might be?

Update: I did some debugging today and found out that the odata call is always triggered and overwrite my changed result!
But how can i prevent this?

Current system is SAP S4HANA 2023 FP03.

Best regards, Wolfgang

entry.title: New SAP Learning Insights podcast episode with Stefanos’ Finance Update is live now!
entry.content:
In this episode, David Chaviano met again with Stefanos Pougkas from SAP to talk about the evolution of SAP's financial offerings. They explore the core functions of SAP Financials, the shift to SAP Public Cloud solutions, and the importance of foundational accounting knowledge for newcomers. Stefanos highlights the impact of AI on automating financial processes and encourages embracing a fit-to-standard philosophy in cloud environments. We conclude by discussing the future of finance consultants amidst evolving market demands, offering valuable insights for anyone looking to excel in SAP finance. And of course, at the end of the episode, Stefanos shares some words of wisdom.

entry.title: Re: RF in Datasphere Unable to fetch delta data Server re...
entry.content:
Hi All,

When you receive the following error message: “Unable to fetch delta data from source, although data is available. Server resources may be overloaded.”

Please check the DHCDCMON transaction in S/4. This transaction allows you to monitor all CDC replication flows. In this transaction you can check the Subscriber Worklist. If any entries are present, this indicates an issue with the replication.

Select the appropriate subscriber ID and restart the status. Once restarted, the delta data should be picked up successfully in SAP Datasphere.

Blog CDC Monitor: https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/datasphere/4549335823.html?locale=en-US 

 

Good luck!

Marinke

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public – Standard Approach for OPEX Budgeting & Control
entry.content:
We would like to seek clarification on SAP’s official and recommended standard approach for managing OPEX budgeting and expenditure control in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Specifically, we would appreciate confirmation on the following:

  1. SAP-Recommended Standard Approach for OPEX
  • What is SAP’s officially recommended approach to manage OPEX budgeting and control in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition today?
  1. Supported Functional Capabilities
    Is there standard, supported functionality available for:
  • Budget availability control
  • Budget checks during Purchase Requisition (PR) and Purchase Order (PO) processing
  • Periodic budget consumption and monitoring reports
  • Budget governance and approval controls
  • Spending control mechanisms
  • Budget control for non-project OPEX expenses without using Project Systems (PS)

This clarification is critical for us, as OPEX budgeting and control is a core financial governance requirement. We want to ensure our implementation:

  • Remains aligned with SAP standard and recommended practices
  • Avoids unnecessary complexity or custom development
  • Supports sustainable operations post go-live

Any guidance or confirmation from SAP or community experts would be greatly appreciated

regards

entry.title: Period Lock Despite Correct MM Config
entry.content:
Help needed! I’m configuring a new Pharma enterprise structure (Company PH01) and hit a stubborn period error during initial stock posting (Mvt 561). Standard MM gates are open, but the transaction is blocked.

The Setup:

  • Company Code: PH01 (V-Pharma India)

  • Fiscal Year Variant: V3 (April-March)

  • Current Action: Posting 1,000 units of V-PARA-500 (Material Type ZMDG)

Audit Trail (Validated):

  • OMSY: Initialized to Period 11 / 2026.

  • MMRV: Confirmed Period 11 is current; back-posting to 10 is allowed.

  • MMPV: Log confirms CoCd is already at 11/2026.

  • MIGO Dates: Both Posting and Document dates set to today (03.02.2026).

  • Line Item: "Item OK" checked; Plant/SLoc PH01 recognized.

The Conflict: Despite MMRV showing Period 11 is open, MIGO returns M7053: "Posting only possible in periods 2026/11 and 2026/10."

entry.title: Re: Manage KPIs and Reports - CDS View contains parameter P_GLAccountHierarchy with / (slash)
entry.content:
Hi David,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's what the CDS view expert replied:

Question1 : Please try to add a parameter P_GLAccountHierarchy in their custom query, rather than pass hard coding value ‘'H109/HONE/H003'. It should like as select from I_MargAnlysProdAndSrvcCube(P_GLAccountHierarchy :$parameters.P_GLAccountHierarchy).

Question2: define transient view entity XXXX  provider contract analytical_query can replace @analytics.query: true if customer’s sap version is supported. And @odata.publish: true is optional not mandatory.

I hope that helps.

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: 12 characters limit on UserID in SU01
entry.content:
We are not able increase the 12 character limit on UserID created in SU01. it is not accepting UserID longer than 12 characters.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: 12 characters limit on UserID in SU01
entry.content:
The user name in SU01 is stored in table USR02 at field BNAME which is defined by data element XUBNAME, CHAR 12. It is used by the Kernel and may not be modified. This is SAP standard and used in many applications. SAP does not support the extension of user ID.

Please read the KBA below in this regards:

1930155 - Is it possible to extend user name length to more than 12 characters or the Alias length to more than 40 characters in SU01

entry.title: Release Navigator for Customer Experience – Q1 2026 Update
entry.content:
Hello CX Community! 

As we begin another year of innovation across the Customer Experience (CX) portfolio, we are excited to share that the release navigator for Customer Experience is now complete for Q1 2026.  

With every new release, a wide range of information, documentation, and resources are updated and made available for you to review. Our goal is to provide timely enablement to help users get ready for the latest release cycle. 

 

Tool Overview

If you are not yet familiar with the release navigator for Customer Experience, it is a resource provided by SAP Enterprise Support designed to offer the following: 

  • A central repository for current product information 
  • A unified access point for all customer experience release materials, including documentation, blogs, webinars, schedules, e-learnings and newsletters 
  • Streamlined access to release details across SAP solutions through additional release navigators 

Content Insights 

Q1 brings updated release information across SAP Sales Cloud, SAP Service Cloud, SAP Engagement Cloud previously known as SAP Emarsys, and SAP Marketing Cloud.  Products following a continuous delivery model: SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 and SAP Commerce Cloud also feature updated Q4 recap content.  

Several important updates have been introduced in this quarter’s release navigator.  

New Release Blog Posts for SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2 

New release blog posts are available for SAP Sales and SAP Service Cloud Version 2. These blogs provide a concise summary of the most significant enhancements delivered each quarter. They are designed to give teams a quick and comprehensive overview of the latest updates. 

Enhancements to the General Page 

The General page within the release navigator has been expanded to include new content. Notably, the SAP Enterprise Support Academy and the CX webcast page have been added, offering users enhanced access to educational resources and support materials directly through the navigator. 

Changes to SAP Marketing Cloud  

Release webcasts for SAP Marketing Cloud will be discontinued as this product is in the process of being retired. Despite this change, SAP remains committed to supporting customers during the transition period by continuing to provide minor enhancement updates. 

Innovation Schedule 

Following the established rhythm from previous years, the release navigator will continue to release content updates quarterly ahead of the planned CX product releases in February, May, August, and November. This ensures you always have the latest information at your fingertips to keep projects running smoothly.  

In addition, new user- experience updates will follow a single annual update cycle, delivered in August along with the Q3 content update. This approach supports a stable and predictable evolution of the platform’s look and feel.  

Informational Timeline: 

 Disclaimer: These dates are for informational purposes only and may change. The Readiness@Scale team has the final decision over any UX modifications.

Connect with Us

Your feedback is essential to shaping future improvements to the release navigator. We invite you to:

  • Complete our feedback survey
  • Share ideas or requests in the comments section
  • Learn more about the Readiness@Scale team
  • Explore additional content

Thank you for your continued engagement and support.

Best Run,
Readiness@Scale

SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: EWM Pick by WO influence source and destination
entry.content:
I would like to create a WO per storage type so that one WO can contain multiple items. Picking should be done via the RF process “Picking by WO” (non‑HU managed).

The standard picking behavior, for example with three WTs in one WO, is:

  • Confirm source for the first WT
  • Confirm source for the second WT
  • Confirm source for the third WT
  • Then confirm all destinations (first, second, third)

Is it possible to change this sequence so that it works as follows instead?

  • Confirm source for the first WT → confirm destination for the first WT
  • Confirm source for the second WT → confirm destination for the second WT
  • Confirm source for the third WT → confirm destination for the third WT

The only option I found that works is to create one WO per WT; however, I need to keep multiple WTs within a single WO.

Please advise

entry.title: Re: About task Create Project Schedule and Budget n the i...
entry.content:
Dear @Amy_Astudillo,

You’re reading it correctly in SAP Cloud ALM, when you create the project, pick the Activate roadmap and set phase dates, you’ve created a high-level timeline. But that’s not yet the same thing as a baselined project schedule + budget in the project-management sense the roadmap task is talking about

Here’s why that roadmap task still exists and what it’s really trying to achieve.

When you select an SAP Activate roadmap in Cloud ALM, you get an actionable task list and you can plan with phases / sprints / milestones and visualize tasks in a Gantt chart

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/gantt-chart-view

That’s a strong start but it’s intentionally template-driven and content-driven:

  • It loads standard roadmap tasks generic to many projects
  • Phase dates are essentially timeboxes containers. They don’t automatically produce a WBS-level network with real dependencies, resource commitments, critical path, etc.

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/solution-transformation-consultant-sap-cloud-alm-delta/running-successful-projects-in-sap-cloud-alm

What the roadmap task Create Project Schedule and Budget is really asking for? That task is a project governance requirement before we start execution, agree on the plan and the money, and baseline them

In practice, it means you must explicitly create and get approval for:

1. A detailed schedule (WBS + dependencies + resources) not just phase dates

A real schedule typically includes things that your initial ALM setup doesn’t automatically define:

  • decomposed work packages per workstream business, data, integrations, security, testing, cutover
  • effort + duration estimates
  • explicit task dependencies so you can see critical path / impacts
  • named resource assignments not just roles and time commitments
  • constraints for example cannot start before fit-to-standard sign-off

Cloud ALM can support much of this once you refine tasks. Gantt view lets you maintain durations and relationships, it also shows effort/story points but you still have to do the planning work it’s not created just by setting phase dates

2. A budget baseline

A project budget is usually more than effort hours on tasks, it includes:

  • implementation partner costs
  • internal backfill
  • training/change/adoption costs
  • tooling testing, migration, integrations, environments, data activities, travel, etc.

Cloud ALM is primarily an ALM/project execution tool, budgeting is often owned by PMO/Finance tooling and processes cost baseline, approvals, change control. The roadmap task exists because every project still needs that baseline, regardless of the execution tool you use

3. Release/upgrade windows and transport freezes must be planned explicitly in Public Cloud

For Public Cloud upgrades are part of life. SAP own onboarding guidance calls out adjusting the plan for releases and avoiding go-lives around upgrade/transport restriction windows. Cloud ALM also highlights release/upgrade windows in its cross-project timeline views, but you still need to incorporate that into your schedule baseline

So do you need a second schedule outside Cloud ALM?

Not necessarily. A better way to interpret the roadmap task is:

  • Ensure a schedule and budget baseline exist and are approved
  • Cloud ALM can be where you maintain the schedule but phase dates alone are not enough

Practical takeaway

The roadmap task exists because SAP Activate is tool-agnostic and is ensuring you don’t confuse:

  • timeboxed phases high-level timeline
    with
  • a baselined schedule + budget governance artifacts used to control scope/time/cost

I hope that my answer will help you

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to Use Custom Fields in Sales Document BAPIs
entry.content:
Hi @CE6, We have added several fields which includes date fields using 'Custom fields' in VBAK. Now we are trying to update the same using BAPI 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE'.  Getting error 'Please enter correct date' from BAPI. Below is the code reference.

DATA: ls_header        TYPE bapisdh1,
        ls_headerx       TYPE bapisdh1x,
        lt_extensionin   TYPE TABLE OF bapiparex,
        ls_extensionin   TYPE bapiparex,
        ls_bape_vbak     TYPE bape_vbak,
        ls_bape_vbakx    TYPE bape_vbakx,
        ls_header_fields TYPE zsds_sales_booking_change.

  DATA lt_bapiparex    TYPE bapiparextab.
  DATA lr_header_ext   TYPE REF TO bape_sdsalesdoc.
  DATA lo_bapi_mapping TYPE REF TO if_cfd_bapi_mapping.
  DATA ls_order_header_inx TYPE bapisdh1x.

  ls_header_fields = im_booking_change.

  " Fill header custom fields
  CLEAR ls_order_header_inx.
  ls_order_header_inx-updateflag = 'U'.

  CREATE DATA lr_header_ext.

  IF ls_header_fields-zz_ocdt IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_ocdt_sdh = sy-datum."ls_header_fields-zz_ocdt.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_ocdt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

IF ls_header_fields-zz_qacertificatedt IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_qacertificatedt_sdh = sy-datum."ls_header_fields-zz_qacertificatedt.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_qacertificatedt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

  IF ls_header_fields-zz_agreementregno IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_agreementsdt_sdh = ls_header_fields-zz_agreementregno.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_agreementsdt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

  IF ls_header_fields-zz_booking_form_dt IS NOT INITIAL.
    lr_header_ext->data-zz1_booking_form_dt_sdh = sy-datum."ls_header_fields-  zz_booking_form_dt.
    lr_header_ext->datax-zz1_booking_form_dt_sdh = 'X'.
  ENDIF.

" Do header custom fields mapping
  lo_bapi_mapping = cl_cfd_bapi_mapping=>get_instance( ).

  TRY.
      lo_bapi_mapping->map_to_bapiparex_single( EXPORTING ir_source_structure = lr_header_ext
                                                CHANGING  ct_bapiparex        = lt_bapiparex ).
    CATCH cx_cfd_bapi_mapping INTO DATA(lx_cfd_bapi_mapping_error).
  ENDTRY.

  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE'
    EXPORTING
      salesdocument    = ls_header_fields-vbeln
*     order_header_in  = ls_header
      order_header_inx = ls_order_header_inx
    TABLES
      return           = lt_return
      extensionin      = lt_bapiparex.

entry.title: New Java Script Event in SAP Analytics Cloud End User File Upload
entry.content:
End User File Upload

In 2024 we shipped the end user file upload in SAP Analytics Cloud planning (see New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024). The feature has been well received by our customers and in widely adopted now. Since then we have been working on enhancing the feature (see New Clean and Replace Feature for SAC End User File Upload ) but also still see areas where we can improve, like the necessity to provide a more flexible un-pivoting (see also How to configure a dynamic file upload in SAP Analytics Cloud).

With version SAC QRC1 2026 we provide a new script event, that allows customers to check and massage the uploaded data before it is written into the system and to control error situations flexibly. 

Events in File Upload

So far we had two Java Script events in the file upload: onBeforeExecute and onAfterExecute. The event onBeforeExecute is executed before any of the upload functions is performed whereas onAfterExecute is triggered when everything has finished already. Customer have been asking for an additional event that is triggered during the upload. This event is now delivered with SAC QRC1 2026 and is called onBeforeImport.

 

When is the Event Called? Which information does it receive?

When running a file upload from the file upload starter the user is asked to select the file in a file selector. Once a file has been chosen the content of the file is uploaded to the browser. 

This is the moment the new event is triggered. In your script you have access to the following information:

  • file name of the chosen file (fileName),
  • the content of the file (fileContent),
  • the target version where the data should be written,
  • value of flag for using the first row as headers (firstRowAsHeaders).

The java script can return the object DataUploadFileHandler. In this object you can set two parameters:

  • continue
  • modifiedFileContent.

How can you react?

The first parameter enables you to abort the file upload. By default the value is set to 'true'. If you set the parameter continue to 'false', then the file upload will be stopped.

The second parameter can contain the modified file content. If you leave this parameter blank, then the system will use the originally uploaded data for further processing. In case the parameter is filled, the system will continue with the new content. That means that in your java script you have the option to not only check the uploaded data but even massage the data before further processing. For example, you could:

  • eliminate erroneous records,
  • correct erroneous records,
  • do dynamic un-pivoting, etc.

Please note that you will receive the data as uploaded with no mappings defined in data wrangling applied yet.

Provided that you do not abort the execution by setting the parameter continue to 'false' the system now proceeds with processing the data. The system will check whether a new file content has been provided - in this case it will proceed with the new content. If no new content has been provided, then the system proceeds with the unaltered initially uploaded content.

The data records are then sent to the data wrangling where all the mappings defined in the definition of the job are applied. Finally the data is checked and the correct data is written to the model.

Sample Coding

We want to give a simple example here. Let us assume we are uploading a file with header line (first row of the file consists of the column headers).

This is our sample coding in our script:

 
//1. send an information with the name of the uploaded file
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Warning, "Uploaded file name is" + fileName );
 
//2. show the content of the uploaded file in the console
for (var i=0;i<fileContent.length;i++){
var lineTest = fileContent[i];
console.log(lineTest);
}
 
//3. massage the uploaded file and only use the first 7 rows (including one header row) for the upload
var UpdatedFile = fileContent.splice(0,7);
var result = cast(Type.DataUploadFileHandler,{continue:true, modifiedFileContent:UpdatedFile});
return result;

In the first block we show an example, how to use the file name of the uploaded file and to display it to the end user /as a warning).

In the second block we show how to access the content of the uploaded file. We loop over the rows and display each row in the console.

In the last block we show how the uploaded data can be modified. We use a modified file content containing only the first 7 rows of the originally uploaded data. As the first row contains the header line we thus only us the first 6 data records from the original file.

Let us run the sample upload with the above script. We trigger the upload starter that is using our script. After our selected file is uploaded to the browser, we get the information which file has been selected.

 Next, we have a look at the console and can view the content of the uploaded data. Our uploaded file containes 10 data records.

And finally, once the upload has finished, we get the information that 6 new records have been written into the selected version.

Thus we can see that indeed the modified data was used that we have stripped down to 6 records (plus the header line).

entry.title: Re: From Meter Read to Bill: Where Utilities Lose Time—and How SAP BTP Fixes It
entry.content:
@lakshmivasa  

Thank you for raising such an important nuance. You’re absolutely right — for many North American utilities, the heavy reliance on diaphragm meters means batch processing won’t disappear overnight. The industry is still in a hybrid reality where AMI-driven real‑time data coexists with legacy meter technologies.

What I’m seeing, though, is that BTP helps bridge this gap rather than waiting for the perfect future state. Even when the field mix is dominated by diaphragm meters, utilities can still use BTP to modernize exception handling, orchestrate events, and gradually shift more processes into near‑real‑time. It becomes a way to “future‑proof” operations so that as AMI penetration grows, the backend is already ready for it.

So yes — the dream isn’t fully realized yet, but BTP is giving utilities a practical path to get there without waiting for 100% smart‑meter adoption. That’s where the real momentum is building.

It seems the error - Client Not Found - is related to OAuth Authentication step...I never did this activation before but this kind of error could be something in the step related to some specifc call to access token. When you pass the wrong credentials, the call will fail. Just my 2 cents as the other error I see related to this is not having the necessary roles...but everyone here said that has the roles...so the error points to something different...the Client ID may be wrong...but just my 2 cents. I am saying that because of this note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003707705 here that has a image (attached here) pointing to a similar error but is very clear in the message that some roles are missing. So if you guys are having the problem but the message doesn't say that is related to roles, but instead, is pointing to a "client not found" error... this is typically the error message that we receive in calls related to OAuth authorizations (mainly in token access).

So that said, maybe a review in the steps can give some light to that case. Otherwise, I suggest open a ticket to better solve that problem.

entry.title: SuccessFactors Onboarding: Improving Form I-9 Document Upload Controls: What Changed & What’s Coming
entry.content:
In early January, we released an update to the Form I-9 workflow that closed a gap in how document uploads were validated during Section 2 completion. We’re now following that update with a new configuration option that gives customers more control over when document uploads are required.

Here’s what changed — and what’s coming next.

The January Update: Preventing Accidental Skips

Previously, it was possible in some scenarios to complete Form I-9 Section 2 without uploading the document(s) presented by the employee. This error could occur when document upload settings were disabled and certain document paths were selected.

While Form I-9 regulations do not require employers to retain copies of documents, many customers choose to do so for audit readiness, internal controls, or E-Verify workflows. Allowing Section 2 to be completed without an expected upload created a risk of inconsistent records and downstream issues; at the same time.

To address this, we released a fix in early January that ensured List A and List B document uploads could not be skipped. This change strengthened process integrity and helped ensure that Section 2 completion aligned with customer expectations and E-Verify requirements.

The Next Step: Giving Customers Control (Coming Soon)

While the January fix improved consistency, we also heard clear feedback from customers:

Document uploads should remain an employer choice — not a system-imposed requirement.

That feedback is correct. Under USCIS Form I-9 guidance, employers may choose whether to retain document copies, and systems should not require uploads unless the employer has elected to do so.

To address this, we are introducing a new configuration toggle that allows customers to control when document uploads are required during the Form I-9 process. This update we are also targeting for an early 2026 patch, and to globally revert our changes made in the early January update. Now, these controls and changes will be behind a configuration toggle.

With this update:

  • Customers can enable or disable document upload requirements based on their own compliance policies.

  • Upload validation will occur only when the toggle is enabled.

  • The system will continue to support scenarios where uploads are optional, while still preventing accidental skips when uploads are required.

This update ensures flexibility without sacrificing compliance — and aligns system behavior with USCIS M-274 and E-Verify guidance.

What This Means for You

  • If you require document uploads today:
    The January update helps ensure uploads aren’t accidentally skipped.

  • If you prefer uploads to remain optional:
    The upcoming toggle will let you configure that behavior explicitly.

  • If you use E-Verify:
    The system continues to support required document information while respecting employer choice around document retention.

Our Guiding Principle

Form I-9 compliance is about inspection and attestation, not mandatory document storage. Our goal is to give customers tools that:

  • Support regulatory compliance

  • Reduce audit risk

  • Respect employer policy decisions

The upcoming configuration option reflects that balance.

If you have questions about how this change applies to your tenant or would like guidance on recommended settings, our support team is happy to help.

entry.title: Tweaks for RAP BO events addressing filtering (before send) and too small payload (callbacks needed)
entry.content:
Are you working on modernizing your SAP integrations, or looking for better ways to integrate with clean core compliance? But when you've looked at "Event Objects" from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you have found that it's not exactly matching your needs? Too many events are fired (can I filter?). The callback API is too time-consuming/unwanted. Or maybe you are simply interested in how the ABAP RESTful Programming Model (RAP) Events work, how you can use and/or extend SAP Standard events?

In this blog I'll go through first some basic info and then start to showcase some tips / tweaks / workarounds you may or may not be aware about. Let's go.

1 ABAP RESTful Programming Model (RAP) Events Intro

I'll keep this very brief as the meat of this blog is to go into the nitty-gritty details. But, for the uninitiated, RAP BO events are events that have been built with the RAP framework which can easily integrate with event brokers, such as SAP Business Technology Platform's Event Mesh or Advanced Event Mesh. You may refer to a previous blog of mine where I go into one full end-to-end example, showcasing how to find SAP standard events and then use them: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end .
The short version is that all events can be found on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub -> Your SAP Product -> Events.

2 Too many events are fired / I want to filter

This is probably one of the most common discussion points when I work with my customers. More often than not a "Created" or even more interestingly, a "Changed" event is simply fired too often for the actual integration needs. So what can we do here? In essence we have 3 main options:

  1. Integration middleware handles filtering (= no changes in SAP system, use event as is)
    1. This can be desirable if there are lots of receiving systems that need to receive information for a certain object, and maybe each of them would like to filter a little different. The middleware receives the event as it happens and can then handle the distribution accordingly. This way 1 single event could be the foundation of several functioning integrations (instead of several point-to-point integrations). The quote-on-quote downside to this is that you would of course increase the complexity in the middleware itself, but again if the same event benefits several receivers, this could be a good option.
  2. Extend SAP standard event to include filtering fields (= extension development in your SAP system, use extended event, not standard)
    1. This can be used if you know for sure you always want to filter out certain types of objects, that none of the integrated systems would want to be informed about. The quote-on-quote downside here is that we need to create an extension. Secondly, we first have to check if the SAP Standard RAP BO in question even allows behavior extensions. If not, we cannot use this option at all.
  3. Use custom event in combination with SAP Standard event as a workaround (= custom development in your SAP system, use custom event, not standard)
    1. Same as for the previous, this option can be used if we know for a fact we always want to filter away certain objects. We can use this over the extend option, if for example the extension was not possible, or if the extension possibilities do not suffice (for example we need to add fields that are not easily associated in the RAP BO, or we need some complex logic and checking before we send an event)

As this blog focuses on RAP, I'll skip the first option and let the integration gurus tackle that one. Let's take a closer look at the other 2...

2.1 Extend SAP standard event

If we found a suitable SAP standard RAP BO event and all we want is to add a few filter fields, we can do that with what is called Derived Events. Please refer to this page for details: Derived Business Events | SAP Help Portal

For my showcase I'll be using R_SalesOrderTP~Changed event. First of, we need to check if the behavior definition of the BO allows extensions. Open it and check for the word "extensible" somewhere at the beginning of the definition. 

If it's there, we can proceed. Next we can also check the event itself and the payload (if no parameter statement is written there, the payload is the key field(s) of the BO alone).

2.1.1 Create derived event payload with filter fields

Before we extend the behavior, we need a custom payload that includes our filter fields. Create a new data definition root entity and define your custom payload. For example:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Sales Ord. ext. event for derived event'
define root view entity ZP_SalesOrder_EventExt as select from I_SalesOrder
{
  key SalesOrder,
  .context.attribute: 'xsapordtype'
  SalesOrderType,
  .context.attribute: 'xsapslsorg'
  SalesOrganization,
  .context.attribute: 'xsapbillplc'
  _BillingPlan.BillingPlanCategory,
  DistributionChannel,
  OrganizationDivision,
  SoldToParty,
  CustomerGroup,
  ShippingCondition,
  IncotermsClassification,
  OverallSDProcessStatus
}

Few points:

  • Key fields must match the RAP BO you are extending
  • You can access fields just like the standard RAP BO and also associated fields (I showcase this with BillingPlanCategory above). Note, we focus only flat structures, so the associated field needs to be 1:1 (for deep structures, please refer to chapter 3).
  • With above, we created exactly 3 filter fields: Sales Order Type, Sales Organization, and Billing Plan Category. Filters must have the annotation "Event.context.attribute" and must start with "xsap"!

2.1.2 Extend standard RAP BO behavior to create a derived event (with our custom payload)

Now we are ready to extend the behavior definition as per usual practice: find/display it in the project explorer - right click - New Behavior Extension. Here type out your derived event, for example:

extension
//using interface i_salesordertp
;
extend behavior for SalesOrder {
  managed event ZZChangedExtended on Changed parameter ZP_SalesOrder_EventExt;
}

Few notes on the above:

  • First of I want to start with the interface commented away above. Bear with me, this is important to understand!
    In RAP it is always best to interact with RAP BOs via their interface, if there is one (not to be confused with a basic interface view, such as I_SalesOrder or I_Customer etc., which reads from the DB table). In the case of R_SalesOrderTP, it has a transactional interface called I_SalesOrderTP. This interface is meant as a stable "API" to interact with the BO, and is usually released with C1/C0 contracts for clean core compliance. So in truth, we should be using the interface as we are extending this BO, but as you see above, it's commented away. That is because we must, in this case. I_SalesOrderTP does not include any of the events, and because we need those, we cannot use the interface in this case. What this means is that my extension behavior definition, as of right now, must be saved as "Standard ABAP" and cannot be assigned to a package with an ABAP Cloud software component. HOWEVER, this is clean core compliant, because R_SalesOrderTP is released with a C0 contract for extension, thus allowing such extensions. The problem is that the syntax check for ABAP for Cloud Development only checks for C1 contracts (which is missing on R_SalesOrderTP behavior), which is why, as of right now, we have to save this as Standard ABAP, meaning a "level B" extension (it would have been "level C", if R_SalesOrderTP hadn't had the C0), which again, is clean core compliant.
  • The rest is quite straight forward. We say which entity we are extending (SalesOrder, using the alias in the behavior definition) and we are stating the Changed event to be extended with ZZChangedExtended with our own payload we previously created.

2.1.3 Create Event Binding for derived event

Next up we need an Event Binding for our derived event. As a small side note: if you only intend to consume your own derived within your SAP system (the same system) and not send it out to an event broker, then you would not need an event binding.
Create a new Event Binding object (Business Service - Event Binding or just search for event binding in the new ABAP object dialog) and add the event we just "created" to it:

Note that we of course refer to the SAP Standard RAP BO, which now includes our derived event thanks to the extension we just did.

2.1.4 Configure Event Channel to include your derived event

Lastly, to send our derived event to an event broker, we need to add it to our event channel. Assuming you already prepared an event channel (refer to my other blog linked above for more info on that), go to /n/IWXBE/CONFIG, select your event channel and go to Outbound Bindings.

Click the create button to add the event binding you just created. It could look something like this:

The above means that my Event Mesh on my BTP subaccount receives the SAP standard Sales Order Created event, and my own derived Sales Order Changed event.

And that's it: now we are ready for filtering or for simply getting the larger payload as per our derived event.

2.1.5 Adding Filters

In the same location as we were (/n/IWXBE/CONFIG -> Event Channel -> Outbound Bindings) click on the button Filters at the top. Open up the folder Outbound Bindings for your event channel and click on your derived event. Clicking on the plus-button shows all available filter fields, in this example:

Create a filter, for example that only Sales Order Type "OR" should be sent to out:

2.1.6 Subscribe to the event on your event broker

To receive this derived event for example in Event Mesh on SAP BTP, you need of course a corresponding queue and then add the subscription for this new derived event. In my example:

2.1.6 Test example

I changed a sales order in my system and saved. This now triggered my derived changed event, which I can see in the events monitoring transaction /n/IWXBE/EVENT_MONITOR (select event channel, click on Outbound Events, double-click on derived event)...

...and on SAP BTP Event Mesh where I added the subscription:

Here we can only see what's sitting in the queue. So let me consume the message in the test tool to show the same payload as in our monitor:

Awesome! We succeeded and this concludes the extend event option.

2.2 Use custom event in combination with SAP Standard event

This is an interesting one and in my experience there has some confusion attached to it. I have heard sentences such as "But can't we just create a custom event instead of the standard one and use that?" And the simple answer to that is yes, but no 😊. Let me explain...

Now of course if we create our own, custom RAP BOs, we can add events to them. That is clear. And within that custom BO you decide when that event is fired. Clear and no problem. But this is all custom.

And now, if you want "your custom event" to be trigged during SAP standard processing, well no, SAP standard can't do that, as it does not know your custom event exists. And if you are thinking to use user exits or BAdIs to do this; think again. That is wrong. User exits and BAdIs are called while the processing has not yet completed. Meaning at this point, in theory, something could still go wrong, but you already triggered your event. So, do not do this, ever! The point of the event framework is to inform about a state change. So you only want to do that, if the state change has actually occurred, meaning after the DB is full updated.

So what else then, you wonder? That's where the SAP standard event comes in (which yes, is fired AFTER the state change occurred and all processing has completed). The SAP Standard event will trigger, and thus if you want more flexibility than just explored in the previous option with the extending of the SAP standard event, that's when we can try to combine standard with custom event.

Essentially, this is the pattern:

  1. SAP standard process does something, which triggers an event for a certain object. This triggering happens at the exact time you want, but the SAP Standard event does not fulfill your requirement (and we can't / don't want to do a derived event)
  2. You will create a custom event on a "dummy" RAP BO which includes the exact payload you need (flat structure for EM, it can be a deep one for AEM).
  3. You will create a local event listener (within your SAP system) for the SAP standard event that triggers at the right moment. Within this local consumption you can now do any ABAP logic you may need. Complex filtering. Complex data collection (calling BAPIs or other things) etc.
    Once all your data validation and collection is done, you fill the payload format of your custom event and finally you trigger your custom event (with a slight workaround, as events can only be raised from with the RAP BO itself)
  4. You only configure your custom event in the event channel for the event broker. The event arriving there is ready to be processed with all needed fields and only events are that are really expected are arriving here

Let's do this example now. We stick with R_SalesOrderTP, this time using Created as the SAP Standard event.

Let's assume I don't just want to send all sales order that are created. Sales order type filtering is not good enough. In fact, it matters whether a product is sold that has certain characteristics, for example. As a simplified example here, I will only check the product type of the product, but hopefully this gives you some ideas; we could check anything at this point.

2.2.1 Create custom event

Our custom event doesn't need much of a BO. Meaning we don't want to (re)create read/update/delete etc. functionality, but this is purely for defining how the event payload should look like and for triggering the custom event. 

In my example I took it a step further: what if I create a generic "event forwarder", that could potentially be used together with many SAP standard objects? Now, this isn't to say that I recommend this approach, in fact quite the opposite. And depending on your exact requirement, this may definitely the wrong approach. But I did this also in an effort to answer if this is at all feasible.

If having an event trigger with only key field information and then having a subsequent callback API later in the integration is a good pattern for you, then this could potentially help, as you could reuse it for several use cases.

But, if instead you want something very specific, for example only forward my object under certain circumstances and including already all data needed, then of course it makes more sense to create a tailored custom event, with the exact payload of that object you want.

So please bear that in mind as we continue with this example. With this example I handle only a generic key-field based payload.

2.2.1.1 Custom entity data definition

We start of course with the data definition. Custom entity is in my opinion the best, as again we don't actually need any read or update functionality, but we simply describe our event payload at this point (and here you could have a large flat/deep structure):

@EndUserText.label: 'Event Forwarder BO'
define root custom entity ZR_EventForwarder
{
  key ObjectKey : abap.string;
  key ObjectType    : zevent_fwd_object;
}

2.2.1.2 Custom entity behavior definition

Next we need the behavior that will include our custom event:

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_r_eventforwarder unique;
strict ( 2 );

define behavior for ZR_EventForwarder
lock master
authorization master ( instance )
{
  field ( readonly ) ObjectKey, ObjectType;
  event ForwardEvent;
}

And the BP implementation: let ADT generate this for you, for we do not need to fill any of the methods (again, we need no read/save methods etc.). So I will not paste the whole Class/or Local Types.

Instead, what we need to do here is now offer an option to "raise" our custom event "ForwardEvent" from outside this BO. The "raise entity event" command is not allowed outside the RAP BO itself. So instead, we need to encapsulate it in a way that ABAP code from outside this behavior pool can call it. For this we need 2 things:

  1. "Local event handler" (not actually inheriting anything)
  2. Method in the global class that can be called from other ABAP code

Let's start with the "local event handler". Within the section Local Types of your behavior implementation class, add something this:

class lcl_event_handler definition friends zbp_r_eventforwarder.
  public section.
    class-methods on_forward_event importing it_events type zbp_r_eventforwarder=>tt_events.
endclass.

class lcl_event_handler implementation.
  method on_forward_event.
    raise entity event zr_eventforwarder~forwardevent from it_events.

  endmethod.
endclass.

And in the Global Class, we change it as follow (full code copied):

class zbp_r_eventforwarder definition public abstract final for behavior of zr_eventforwarder.
  types tt_events type table for event zr_eventforwarder~forwardevent.

  public section.
    constants:
      co_type_salesorder    type zevent_fwd_object value 'SALESORDER'.
    class-methods raise_forward_event
      importing it_events   type tt_events.


endclass.

class zbp_r_eventforwarder implementation.
  method raise_forward_event.
    " tell RAP framework that we are in a SAVE sequence (otherwise it thinks
    " we are in the middle of a MODIFY)
    cl_abap_tx=>save( ).
    " trigger event (only allowed during SAVE)
    lcl_event_handler=>on_forward_event( it_events ).
  endmethod.
endclass.

We did 3 main things: a global type for the event payload table, a constant to distinguish different types of RAP BO that I mean to handle with my generic one (right now only one) and, most importantly, a global class method that can be called from other ABAP code.

2.2.1.3 Event Binding

As before, we need an event binding if we want to forward this to an event broker:

(Namespace, object type etc. can be freely chosen.)

And that's all for our custom event. Remember that the only purpose of this custom event is to offer a way to define the payload and to trigger it. Any functionality, such as filtering, complex validation or data selection etc. we do when the real SAP standard event is triggered. Meaning in the local consumption. 

2.2.2 Local consumption of SAP Standard event

Now I won't go into step-by-step on this one, as I have done this on the above linked blog post of mine. Please check there if the below steps are not enough for you.

I have an ABAP Class, abstract, final with the "for events of" functionality, where once again we must type R_SalesOrderTP here (and not I_SalesOrderTP, which would have been the preferred option). This means my class has to be again saved as "Standard ABAP" and cannot be in an ABAP for Cloud Development package. But this is clean core compliant, as explained above.

In Local Types I have my local class that inherits from cl_abap_behavior_event_handler and I add a method for the Created event:

class lcl_event_cons definition inheriting from cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.

  private section.
    methods:
      consume_created for entity event instances for salesorder~created.
endclass.

And here the implementation of that method (we'll go through it one by one below):

    data: lt_fwd_event  type zbp_r_eventforwarder=>tt_events.
    
    " go through RAP BO entity instances that triggerd the event
    " (should only be 1 as we listend to sales order (header) created)
    loop at instances into data(instance).
      " get data needed for complex filtering
      select salesorderitem, product, \_product-producttype
        from i_salesorderitem
        where salesorder = @instance-salesorder
        into table (lt_items).

      " check if sales order should be handled (forwarded to event broker)
      if line_exists( lt_items[ producttype = 'HAWA' ] ). 
        " this sales order is now filtered OK and will be added to the forwarding instances
        append initial line to lt_fwd_event assigning field-symbol(<fs_event>).
        <fs_event> = value #( objectkey  = instance-salesorder
                              objecttype = zbp_r_eventforwarder=>co_type_salesorder ).
      else.
        continue. " we are not interested in this Sales Order
      endif.
    endloop.

    " now that we checked all instances, only those that we filtered will be forwarded
    if lt_fwd_event is not initial.
      zbp_r_eventforwarder=>raise_forward_event( lt_fwd_event ).
    endif.
  • We create an internal table of the payload type that is the custom event (we created a global type in the behavior definition)
  • We loop through all instances that were sent by the SAP standard event (since this is a sales order (header), this will only ever include 1)
  • As we loop I select some additional data to do my "complex logic" for checking if this sales order should be forwarded or filtered out.
  • Once all my requirements are met for forwarding the sales order, I start to fill the custom event payload table.
  • When the looping is done, we check if any sales orders remained (if we have any custom event payload) and if yes, we now call the method we offered on the custom event RAP BO behavior as global class method and give it the payload we just filled.

 And that's all 😲😀

2.2.3 Finishing

As before, we need to configure our custom event (binding) in the event channel in order to forward it to the event broker:

On the event broker, we need to subscribe to that topic, to receive it there:

To summarize, this is what will happen:

  1. A user creates any sales order
  2. R_SalesOrderTP-Created is triggered
  3. Our local consumption of the SAP standard event is called: our local consumption checks if the sales order has any items with products of product type HAWA. If yes, we add it to be forwarded. If no, we disregard it.
  4. If we had any sales order to forward, the ZR_EventForwarder behavior is called via workaround (a little bit nasty by just calling the global class but I could not think of any other way. For example we can't use an action, as actions are invoked via EML MODIFY, which in turn does not allow for RAISE EVENT). This triggers the ZR_EventForwarder-EventForwarded event with our payload
  5. The custom event is forwarded to event broker

2.2.4 Testing

Let's test this. I create a new sales order and I include 1 item that has a product of type HAWA. Meaning my filtering will allow this sales order and eventually trigger my custom event. 

Let's see what the event monitor says:

And consuming this message in BTP Event Mesh:

3 I want a larger payload

This is without a doubt the 2nd most discussed topic when talking event based integrations. In order to avoid callback APIs, maybe this is the way to go for you. As of right now, SAP Standard delivers notification events only, meaning small payloads to allow for the creation of fast, simple, integrations that can be easily reused.

You may already noticed that we already covered this in chapter 2. Both when extending the SAP standard event or when creating a custom event to work in combination with standard: I showcased how we can decide our own payload. So far I have only mentioned flat structures, as that is the only type that is supported by Event Mesh on SAP BTP. If you have a need for deep structure payloads, you may want to consider to upgrade to Advanced Event Mesh instead. And instead of creating a brand new example, please check out this blog post by a colleague that goes into deep payload: RAP Events with AEM 3 - Creating Events with deep payload.

Conclusion / Final Words

I hope this blog post helps and gives some ideas how far you can take RAP BO events and influence your integration landscape, in a positive way (of course, that's the end goal).

Is there anything I've missed? Another requirement that you would like to solve with eventing? Or any other questions? Let me know in the comments and let's see 😊

I want to clarify that it's not a queue issue because no documents appear stuck in the queue. And the LE delivery is visible after unload or packing the product from /SCWM/RFUI in EWM side

1 - Inboound delivery EWM # 27 created in EWM Side. The SAP message indicates that delivery LE 6000019 was created. (Image1)

EWM - Goods Movement

entry.title: Re: ODBC connection to SAP HANA HDI container fails with service key credentials
entry.content: Cause The password contains one of the following special characters: []{}(),;?*=!@ . Resolution When using any of the characters: []{}(),;?*=!@ in a value, the value must be wrapped in curly braces, for example {val;ue}. If you are using the } character in a value (namely the username or password), then the application must format it correctly in the SQLDriverConnect string by specifying it twice, for example {pass}}word}.

entry.title: Re: ODBC connection to SAP HANA HDI container fails with...
entry.content:
Extracted from Note:

Cause
The password contains one of the following special characters: []{}(),;?*=!@ .

Resolution
When using any of the characters: []{}(),;?*=!@ in a value, the value must be wrapped in curly braces, for example {val;ue}. If you are using the } character in a value (namely the username or password), then the application must format it correctly in the SQLDriverConnect string by specifying it twice, for example {pass}}word}.

entry.title: Re: Problems with SAP Crystal Reports 2025
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Please be reminded that SAP Crystal Reports 2020 and SAP Crystal Reports 2025 are 64 bit, and older versions are 32 bit.

You will need to upgrade the database drivers related to the databases used in your reports from 32 to 64 bit on your machine for your reports to work correctly.

Kind regards

Dominik

entry.title: Updates from B1 Support
entry.content:
2026 is well on it's way, already February. Here are some short updates from B1 Support.

We have held our first session in our highly successful Webinar Series. The topic this time was Understanding and analyzing Logfiles. The session was delivered and prepared by two absolute experts in the area - Branislav Stark presented the material that he had prepared with Product Area Lead Yvonne Doyle.

The recording and the material can now be found in the KBA – 3525031 - Central KBA for Partner Enablement Sessions delivered by SAP Business One Support  In the KBA you will find the recordings for all 9 sessions that has been delivered to date.

Currently we are preparing for the 10th session in the series - Managing certificates in SAP Business One. The session will go ahead on March 4th, 14.00 CET. Product Area Lead Pavol Hrasko will present assisted by members of his team. 

The registration is now open, the sessions are for partners and they are for free. Please follow the link to register - SAP Partner Portal | Managing certificates in SAP Business One. 

Please note, as a customer or a freelance, you can access the KBA and take in the material that way. If you are a Spanish speaker or have people in your team that prefer consuming the sessions in Spanish, we are planning some this spring. Keep an eye out on linkedin and the portal. 

Moving on

In the coming days FP 2602 will be released. Keep an eye out in the usual places. 

That time of year

This is repeat, but we do believe you will benefit from getting this served again. Period End Closing Material. 

There is a lot of different materials that SAP has made available, ranging from basic how-to stuff to more intricate issues that you might run into.

Starting with the basics - How to Perform a Year-End Closing in SAP Business One 10.0 – it takes you through the process of how to prepare and execute the period end in SAP Business One.  

If you are already mastering this topic, then the Guided Answer (GA) for Period End Closing – covers a whole range of areas, a good friend to have when doing the closing. Also, for the experienced user.

Another GA that might come in handy is the one for Financial Statements – again, a useful resource to have available should you not agree with some of the numbers that are presented.

While we like the Guided Answer format and hope you do too, if you prefer to consume the material in the form of an SAP Note, we have a fairly detailed one for you as well - 2940618 - Troubleshooting: Period-End Closing Issues.

The note takes you through issues caused by Settings, User Handling, Database issues and some System Limitations. Much of it, you will also find in the GA previously mentioned.

Last

Short reminder about our Guided Answers. SAP Business One Support has of today released 31 different GAs. You will find all of them linked to the central KBA - 3213852 - Central KBA for SAP Business One Guided Answers

The Support Spotlight Blogs – find them here: Tips from SAP Business One Support

Keep interacting in the communities -  SAP Business One Community

entry.title: Re: Can Crystal Reports 2025 run on BOE 4.3 or is Busines...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

We always recommend version parity.

For SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 you will need to run SAP Crystal Reports 2025 (64 bit) and for SAP BusinessObjects 4.3 you will need to run SAP Crystal Reports 2020 (64 bit).

Kind regards

Dominik

entry.title: New Espresso Session: Introducing the Utilities Customer Self-Service Agent on February 19
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community, 

Discover the newly released Utilities Customer Self-Service Agent, a groundbreaking solution designed to address end-customer needs through human-like and effective interactions, to deliver 24/7 support, reduce service costs, and strengthen customer relationships.

This AI-powered agent integrates seamlessly with SAP S/4HANA Utilities, enabling personalized customer experiences with full context awareness, covering contracts, tariffs, products, consumption, and in future even up-selling opportunities and other proactive engagements.

 

 

What You’ll Learn in This Session:

  • Solution Overview: Explore core capabilities and integration with SAP S/4HANA Utilities—and see how it transforms customer engagement.
  • Demo: Experience a hands-on demo of the agent and discover its powerful features in action.
  • Implementation Insights: Gain expert guidance on technical requirements and strategies for a smooth, successful rollout.

Why Should Join?

This session is designed for utility companies, customer service managers, IT leads, and SAP solution architects who want to enhance customer engagement and optimize service operations.

Register here and join Bruno Pincolini in this session.

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM RF Exception Handling - Deep Dive with BIDP- Partial Pick denial example
entry.content: Great topic! Question, in theory can I use fm /SCWM/TO_PREP_WHR_UI_INT to create Warehouse Tasks and Orders given a list of HUs from an Inbound Delivery?
